$NVDA is reportedly in talks with SoftBank to help build made-in-Japan AI servers.

SoftBank is weighing plans to design and assemble homegrown AI server components by the end of the decade.
$GOOGL says Fitbit Air will launch at $99.99 with Health Coach included through Health Premium.

My full $DDOG earnings takeaway can be found here:

piped.video/7EBtm9TFiRU?si=Mvea…

$DDOG delivered one of the strongest software quarters in a while with NRR reaccelerating to 121% and reminding the market that software isn't dead.

Agentic AI is making infrastructure more complex since companies juggle multiple models, multi-cloud workloads, GPU fleets, token costs and production reliability.

That creates a massive monitoring problem and Datadog monetizes that complexity.

A $10K investment in $MU at the IPO would be worth ~$4.6M today.

Getting that return required surviving multiple 50%+ drawdowns along the way.
$QQQ IS ABOVE $700 FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER

$PLTR has generated ~$1.7B in U.S. government contract obligations in the past 16 months.

That nearly matches the entire 5-year cumulative total from 2020 to 2024.

$HIMS launched Labs AI which is its first AI care agent for personalized health insights and nuanced health questions.

The company says Labs AI is built with guardrails and uses a custom AI system to support Hims & Hers care platform.
$DDOG up over 30% today after it reminds the market that software isn't dead  🙃

$AMZN & $USB expanded their AWS partnership to migrate hundreds of mission-critical banking apps across payments, banking & wealth management

U.S. Bank will also use AWS to build GenAI-powered customer service across voice, chat & SMS for ~13M consumers & 1.4M businesses.

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang was asked about a wealth tax that could cost him $8B.

He said “I prefer lower taxes, but I love this country and don’t mind paying” and joked he’d personally fix the potholes on the 101 if they let him.

All portfolio performance & transaction data have been independently validated by an independent third party:

savvytrader.com/StockSavvySh…

Two months ago I found a hidden AI hardware name under $10B that had massive exposure to AI data centers, optical networking & the new age of defense.

My thesis was as systems become faster, more distributed & mission critical then precision timing becomes one of the bottlenecks that determines whether the whole system works.

After today’s move, the stock has nearly tripled in less than two months. Any guesses?

$AMZN custom silicon business hit a $20B annualized run rate in Q1 2026 growing triple digits YoY.

CEO Andy Jassy framed the chip business as a $50B standalone equivalent which would place Amazon larger than $AVGO AI chip franchise ($34B annualized) & $AMD entire data center segment ($23B annualized).

Amazon's custom silicon business is now one of the top three data center chip franchises in the world built entirely without selling chips externally.

$AMZN Pharmacy is expanding access to $NVO oral Ozempic with same-day prescription delivery across nearly 3,000 U.S. cities.

Amazon also plans to add One Medical pickup kiosks and expand delivery to nearly 4,500 cities by year-end.

📺 THIS IS A POWER PLAY MARKET RIGHT NOW

We had a number of high-conviction, repeatable setups in the past several weeks.

1. Apple $AAPL

We had an initial signal + post-earnings pivot re-entry. Apple is still in play. Key level – an all-time high at ~$288. The stock is “knocking on the door” of a breakout.

I believe Apple will likely trade at ~$295+ in the near term (next week-ish).

2. Google $GOOGL

Google has been one of the best trades in a long time for me. We bought around earnings, positioning ourselves early.

Google keeps paying: I held it overnight, capturing a gap move (~+$5).

Following signals early → front-running momentum.

3. Tesla $TSLA

Tesla is the latest addition to Power Plays. It began with a Red Dog Reversal entry below $360, reclaiming the level. It was a trigger to go long.

Risk was tightly defined: a stop was placed around $364, which was never hit.

#TSLA started as a normal long → scaled into a “power play” position. Added more size on confirmation days.

Price ran to around $420, delivering strong upside. A later Red Dog Reversal sell signal → used to trim (reduce size), not fully exit.

Always trim when something is big (Tesla was up ~6% → partial sell). It maintains exposure while locking in gains.

I also monetized the trade in $TSLA in different ways: stock, $TSLL, selling puts (collecting premium).

Tesla's narrative may finally shift.

With the launch of options on the KraneShares CSI China Internet UCITS ETF $KWEB on Eurex, European investors now have the potential opportunity to capitalise on volatility in the China Internet and AI theme, enabling enhanced income generation and downside protection.

Eurex interviews James Maund, KraneShares Head of Capital Markets, Henry Reece, KraneShares Director of Business Development, and Zubin Ramdarshan, Global Co-Head of Derivatives Products and Markets at Eurex, to discuss the significance of the new listing. 

Read the full interview here: rebrand.ly/c22e64

#MarketingCommunication

Bitcoin's most realistic bear case is an impossible situation.

The US government would have to reduce its $39 trillion debt pile, but policymakers have never managed to do that.

This makes the long-term thesis for $BTC bulletproof, as @DavidDziekanski explains.

We're pleased to announce the listing of EUR and GBP hedged share classes of the KraneShares CSI China Internet UCITS ETF on the @LSEplc, under tickers $KWEH (EUR Hedged) and $KWBH (GBP Hedged).

These new share classes give European investors greater flexibility to access China's leading internet and technology companies, including $BABA, @TencentGlobal, $BIDU, and @Meituan, while managing currency exposure in today's volatile macro environment.

Read the full press release: rebrand.ly/5faf3c
